title: Republic Act No. 8011
text:
H. No. 156

Republic of the Philippines Congress of the Philippines Metro Manila

Third Regular Session

Begun and held in Metro Manila, on Monday, the twenty-fifth day of July, nineteen hundred and ninety-four.

[REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8011]

AN ACT CHANGING THE NAME OF THE LEYTE STATE SCHOOL OF ARTS AND TRADES IN TANAUAN, LEYTE, TO LEYTE COLLEGE OF ARTS AND TRADES

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The name of the Leyte State School of Arts and Trades in Tanauan, Leyte, is hereby changed to Leyte College of Arts and Trades.

SEC. 2. The Leyte College of Arts and Trades shall be under the supervision of the Commission on Higher Education.

SEC. 3. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.) EDGARDO J. ANGARA President of the Senate

(Sgd.) JOSE C. DE VENECIA, JR. Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on February 28, 1995 and February 27, 1995, respectively.

(Sgd.) EDGARDO E. TUMANGAN Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.) CAMILO L. SABIO Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved:

FIDEL V. RAMOS President of the Philippines

Lapsed into the law on May 25, 1995, without the signature of the President, in accordance with Article VI, Section 27(1) of the Constitution.

Source : Presidential Management Staff
